title: High Intensity Outpatient Program (HIOP)
organization_name: Another Chance Drug & Alcohol Treatment Center
description: Structured outpatient addiction treatment with 4–6 hours of services per day, 5 days a week, including individual and group therapy, medical staff, meals, and family counseling.
engage: Call 503-747-0404 or email info@anotherchancerehab.com to start the admissions process. Programs available at two Portland locations.
content:
Services

  • Individual Therapy: One-on-one sessions with a licensed counselor.
  • Group Therapy: Peer-based sessions focused on recovery skills.
  • Medical Staff On-Site: Medical supervision available during program hours.
  • Recreational Therapy: Activity-based therapeutic programming.
  • Family Counseling: Optional family involvement in treatment.
  • Drug & Alcohol Screening: Regular testing throughout the program.
  • Meals Provided: Meals included during program hours.

Who It's For

  • Individuals with substance use disorders needing intensive structure without 24-hour supervision
  • Those with co-occurring mental health issues alongside addiction

Program Length

Typically 1 week to 1 month, tailored to individual progress. Often followed by step-down to IOP.

Locations

  • 12670 NW Barnes Rd STE 200, Portland, OR 97229
  • 211 NE 18th Ave, Portland, OR 97232

title: Intensive Outpatient Program (IOP)
organization_name: Another Chance Drug & Alcohol Treatment Center
description: Outpatient addiction treatment with 3 hours of therapy per day, 3–5 days a week, including individual and group sessions and optional evening groups for those managing work or family commitments.
engage: Call 503-747-0404 or email info@anotherchancerehab.com to start the admissions process. Evening groups available.
content:
Services

  • Individual Therapy: Personalized counseling sessions.
  • Group Therapy: Peer support and skill-building groups.
  • Evening Groups: Optional evening sessions for flexible scheduling.
  • Family Counseling: Optional family involvement.
  • Drug & Alcohol Screening: Regular testing throughout treatment.
  • Coping & Relapse Prevention Skills: Practical tools for maintaining sobriety.

Who It's For

  • Individuals who have completed inpatient or PHP treatment and need continued structured support
  • Those balancing work, school, or family while maintaining recovery

Program Length

Typically 1 to 45 days depending on individual progress.

Locations

  • 12670 NW Barnes Rd STE 200, Portland, OR 97229
  • 211 NE 18th Ave, Portland, OR 97232

title: Outpatient Program (OP)
organization_name: Another Chance Drug & Alcohol Treatment Center
description: Low-intensity outpatient addiction treatment with 1–3 hours of therapy per week, including individual and group sessions, for individuals with established recovery stability reintegrating into daily life.
engage: Call 503-747-0404 or email info@anotherchancerehab.com. Evening groups available Monday–Wednesday.
content:
Services

  • Individual Therapy: Weekly one-on-one counseling sessions.
  • Group Therapy: Peer accountability and support groups.
  • Evening Groups: Monday–Wednesday evening options.

Who It's For

  • Individuals who have completed higher levels of care (PHP or IOP)
  • Those needing flexible scheduling while maintaining accountability
  • People ready to reintegrate into daily routines with ongoing therapeutic support

Program Length

Approximately 30 days.

Locations

  • 12670 NW Barnes Rd STE 200, Portland, OR 97229
  • 211 NE 18th Ave, Portland, OR 97232

title: Substance Abuse Counseling
organization_name: Another Chance Drug & Alcohol Treatment Center
description: Individual and group substance abuse counseling using cognitive-behavioral therapy, mindfulness, and skills development, tailored to all stages of recovery from intensive support to maintenance.
engage: Call 503-747-0404 or email info@anotherchancerehab.com to schedule an initial assessment and begin counseling.
content:
Services

  • Individual Therapy: One-on-one CBT-based counseling sessions.
  • Group Therapy: Peer-based group sessions.
  • Mindfulness & Meditation: Practices integrated into treatment.
  • Nutrition & Health Guidance: Holistic wellness support.
  • Community Resource Referrals: Connections to sober living and peer support groups.
  • Ongoing Recovery Support: Follow-up sessions after primary treatment.

Who It's For

Individuals at any stage of recovery from substance use disorders, from those needing intensive support to those maintaining long-term sobriety.

Locations

  • 12670 NW Barnes Rd STE 200, Portland, OR 97229
  • 211 NE 18th Ave, Portland, OR 97232

title: DUII Program
organization_name: Another Chance Drug & Alcohol Treatment Center
description: Substance abuse treatment program for individuals charged with DUII (Driving Under the Influence of Intoxicants) in Oregon, including flexible scheduling to meet court-required hours, random testing, and progress reporting.
engage: Call 503-747-0404 or email info@anotherchancerehab.com to enroll. Programs accommodate both diversion and conviction cases.
content:
Services

  • Flexible Scheduling: Sessions designed to meet court-required treatment hours.
  • Treatment for Diversion & Conviction Cases: Programs tailored to first-time and repeat offenders.
  • Random Drug & Alcohol Testing: Regular screening throughout the program.
  • Progress Reporting: Reports provided to legal representatives or the court.
  • Continued Support: Ongoing support options beyond the initial treatment period.

Who It's For

Individuals charged with a DUII in Oregon, including first-time offenders and those with repeat offenses.

Program Length

Most participants complete the program within 3 to 4 months.

Locations

  • 12670 NW Barnes Rd STE 200, Portland, OR 97229
  • 211 NE 18th Ave, Portland, OR 97232

title: Substance Abuse Evaluations & Assessments
organization_name: Another Chance Drug & Alcohol Treatment Center
description: Professional substance abuse evaluations conducted by licensed specialists for court orders, employment requirements, or personal reasons. Includes detailed substance use history, psychosocial assessment, and treatment recommendations.
engage: Call 503-747-0404 or email info@anotherchancerehab.com to schedule an evaluation appointment.
content:
Services

  • Substance Use History Review: Detailed assessment of types, frequency, and duration of use.
  • Psychosocial Assessment: Evaluation of mental health and social factors.
  • Physical Health Review: Medical condition screening.
  • Behavioral Analysis: Patterns and risk behavior assessment.
  • Optional 30-Day Evaluation: Includes random urine testing and collateral information gathering.
  • Written Results & Recommendations: Summary, treatment recommendations, and referrals if needed.

Who It's For

  • Individuals required to complete an assessment for a court order
  • Those needing documentation for employment purposes
  • Anyone seeking a personal substance use evaluation

Cost

  • Assessment Only (no treatment): $400
  • Written results delivery: $300

Locations

  • 12670 NW Barnes Rd STE 200, Portland, OR 97229
  • 211 NE 18th Ave, Portland, OR 97232

title: Loved One's Education & Support Group
organization_name: Another Chance Drug & Alcohol Treatment Center
description: 12-week education and support group for family members and friends of people in addiction recovery, meeting every Tuesday at 5pm. Covers addiction science, healthy boundaries, and relationship skills.
engage: Call 503-747-0404 or email info@anotherchancerehab.com to register. Groups meet every Tuesday at 5:00 PM.
content:
Services

  • 12-Week Curriculum: Structured education covering addiction science, recovery, and family dynamics.
  • Emotional Support: Peer community among families navigating a loved one's addiction.
  • Resource Sharing: Referrals to Al-Anon, SMART Recovery Family & Friends, and other groups.
  • Relationship Skills: Final session dedicated to rebuilding healthy relationships.

Who It's For

Family members and friends of individuals struggling with addiction or in recovery from substance use disorders.

Schedule

Every Tuesday at 5:00 PM for 12 weeks.

Locations

  • 12670 NW Barnes Rd STE 200, Portland, OR 97229
  • 211 NE 18th Ave, Portland, OR 97232

title: SMART Recovery
organization_name: Another Chance Drug & Alcohol Treatment Center
description: Science-based addiction recovery program using cognitive-behavioral therapy to help participants manage cravings, handle difficult emotions, and build sustainable recovery skills as an alternative to 12-step models.
engage: Call 503-747-0404 or email info@anotherchancerehab.com to learn about current group schedules and availability.
content:
Services

  • CBT-Based Group Sessions: Evidence-based cognitive behavioral therapy in a group format.
  • Individual Therapy: One-on-one support integrated with the program.
  • Mental Health Support: Addresses co-occurring mental health concerns.
  • Craving Management Tools: Practical strategies for managing urges.
  • Emotion Regulation: Skills for handling difficult feelings without substances.
  • Routine Building: Support for creating stable, recovery-supportive daily habits.

Who It's For

  • Individuals seeking a science-based, self-directed recovery approach
  • Those who prefer alternatives to traditional 12-step programs
  • People wanting practical, skill-building tools for daily life

Locations

  • 12670 NW Barnes Rd STE 200, Portland, OR 97229
  • 211 NE 18th Ave, Portland, OR 97232

title: Supportive Housing
organization_name: Another Chance Drug & Alcohol Treatment Center
description: Connections to sober living homes through a partnership with Northwest Recovery Homes, providing structured drug-free housing with accountability, random testing, and daily routines for people leaving addiction treatment.
engage: Call 503-747-0404 or email info@anotherchancerehab.com to discuss housing placement options after completing treatment.
content:
Services

  • Sober Living Placement: Referrals to partner sober living homes through Northwest Recovery Homes.
  • Structured Environment: House rules, accountability, and daily routines.
  • Random Drug Testing: Regular screening to maintain a drug-free household.
  • Community Support: Peer community among residents in recovery.
  • Life Skills Development: Personal responsibility and independent living skills.

Who It's For

Individuals transitioning out of intensive addiction treatment who need a structured, drug-free living environment before returning to fully independent living.

Locations

  • 12670 NW Barnes Rd STE 200, Portland, OR 97229
  • 211 NE 18th Ave, Portland, OR 97232
